checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All text present in the JSON can be traced back to the source PDF. Meta-information like table captions and summaries, as well as the omission notice, are categorized as acceptable AI-generated additions or indicators of data loss rather than content fabrication.

I verified all contact-related placeholders and text. The JSON correctly includes placeholders found in the source (e.g., '[INSERT AGENCY CONTACT]') and does not invent any phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs.

A significant portion of the document is missing from the output. The JSON includes an explicit note that 11 sections were omitted. Specifically, major policy sections such as RA-1 (Policy and Procedures), RA-2 (Security Categorization), RA-3 (Risk Assessment), and RA-5 (Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning) are entirely absent. These sections span pages 2-10 and 13-17 of the source PDF.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains full sections for RA-1 (pages 2-3), RA-2 (pages 6-7), RA-3 (pages 7-10), and RA-5 (pages 13-17) which include critical policy requirements, procedures, and technical standards.

checks.a_analysis: For the sections that were successfully converted, the tables preserve the correct number of rows. The Responsibilities table correctly aggregates roles from pages 1 and 2, and the Impact Definitions and matrix tables on page 11 are complete.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The sections that are present (Scope, Material Superseded, Responsibilities, Use of Independent Assessors, etc.) are converted with high fidelity and do not suffer from internal truncation. Omissions are handled at the whole-section level (C3).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the 'Impact Definitions' table, specifically in the 'Low' impact cell, a closing parenthesis present in the source is missing in the output. This slightly alters the grouping of the parenthetical list of examples compared to the original text.
checks.b_evidence_source: (including mission, functions, image or reputation), agency assets, or individuals;
checks.b_evidence_output: (including mission, functions, image or reputation, agency assets, or individuals;
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 'Impact Definitions', table 'Impact Definitions', 'Low' row definition cell

checks.a_analysis: The signature block on page 21, consisting of the lines 'Printed Name of Secretary/CIO or Designee [Date]' and 'Signature of Secretary/CIO or Designee', appears only once in the source document. However, in the JSON output, these two text blocks are duplicated within the elements of Section 19 (appearing as elements 14/15 and again as elements 18/19).
checks.b_evidence_source:
Printed Name of Secretary/CIO or Designee [Date]
Signature of Secretary/CIO or Designee
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "_______________________________________ ________________\nPrinted Name of Secretary/CIO or Designee [Date]" ... "text": "_______________________________________ \nSignature of Secretary/CIO or Designee" (Repeated twice)

checks.a_analysis: The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The document title is assigned H2, as are the major section titles (Scope, Material Superseded, etc.). Subsections like 'Use of Independent Assessors' are correctly nested as H3. No heading levels are skipped.

All tables identified in the source (Responsibilities, Impact Definitions, Risk Matrix, etc.) are correctly rendered in the JSON. The number of columns matches the source, and header rows are correctly designated.

The reading order for content derived from page 21 is incorrect. In the source, the document header metadata ('Risk Assessment Policy...', 'Page No. 21 of 21') appears at the top of the page, followed by the signature lines. In the JSON, the signature lines (element 14/15) are placed before this header text (element 17), and then the signature lines are repeated again (element 18/19). This split and reordering of unique page elements constitutes a structural failure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 21: [Header: Risk Assessment Policy... Page No. 21 of 21] followed by [Signature Block]
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 19: Element 14 (Signature Line) -> Element 17 (Header Text) -> Element 18 (Signature Line)

checks.a_analysis: Lists are converted faithfully. Lettered lists are rendered as unordered lists but retain the original character labels ('a.', 'b.', etc.) within the text, preserving the intended meaning and structure. Checkboxes in Appendix A are logically converted into lists.
No evidence of link injection or fabrication. Plain text URLs in the source are correctly captured in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The document follows the same sequential flow of sections (Scope, Material Superseded, Responsibilities, RA policies, and Appendices). The multi-page PDF layout is converted into a single-column responsive HTML layout, which is an expected transformation. The header metadata (Document No, Dates, Version) from the PDF pages has been moved to a metadata table at the end of the document, which preserves the information while improving the reading flow in a web context.

All tables from the source PDF are correctly rendered as HTML tables. This includes complex tables like the 'Threat Likelihood and Overall Risk Matrix' on page 11, which maintains its grid structure and calculation logic, and the various reporting tables in Appendix A (pages 18-20). The columns and rows are well-aligned, headers are distinct, and the content is clearly readable.

The meaningful image in the document, the State of North Carolina seal found in the PDF page headers, is present in the HTML output at the top of the page and within the document metadata section at the bottom. No meaningful images are missing or broken.
A visual inspection of the rendered HTML shows no instances of duplicated content blocks, paragraphs, or tables. Each section appears once in its correct logical order according to the source PDF.
